projects.name: channel-manager
projects.progressLabel: broad platform scaffolding and documentation exist, with an active refactor underway in backend integrations and tenant admin
currentGoal: Stabilize the current backend refactor, verify the solution still builds cleanly, and bring the documentation back in line with the actual codebase state.
doneRecently: Built a large modular-monolith hotel platform with backend modules, Docker setup, CI workflows, and a frontend scaffold.
Added detailed architecture docs such as `README.md`, `DELIVERY_CHECKLIST.md`, `IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md`, and `FILE_INDEX.md`.
Added or refined many OTA adapter, payments, and tenant-admin pieces.
inProgress: The working tree has active changes across:
Current diff stats show a substantial simplification/refactor rather than a tiny patch.
nextUp: Build and test the backend after the current refactor, then capture the exact failures if any remain.
Review the "100% complete" documentation claims and downgrade or nuance them where reality is still evolving.
Add a short agent-facing handoff section to the main docs if this repo will keep being worked on by multiple Codex sessions.
blockers: The docs currently present the platform as fully complete, but the working tree shows active architectural churn.
Future agents could trust the completion docs too literally and miss the current refactor context.
`.env` exists locally, so environment-sensitive work should avoid exposing secrets in commits or notes.
repoNotes: Current dirty files are concentrated in API wiring, adapters, payment integration, and tenant-admin modules.

narrative.currentGoal: Stabilize the current backend refactor, verify the solution still builds cleanly, and bring the documentation back in line with the actual codebase state.
narrative.inProgress: The working tree has active changes across:

projects.name: pawPetrol
projects.progressLabel: two related Python video-generation pipelines exist; one is a prototype and one is under active iteration
currentGoal: Consolidate the project direction around the strongest episode-generation pipeline and get one end-to-end content run working reliably.
doneRecently: `detective-biscuit-factory`: a simpler episode pipeline with story, scene planning, images, narration, subtitles, audio mix, and export stages
`detective_dog_ai`: a more advanced content engine with character profiles, scene graphing, retention hooks, timing control, overlays, and export
`detective_dog_ai` shows active work on multi-character support, story generation, validation, subtitles, and ComfyUI integration.
inProgress: The project appears to be evolving from the simpler Biscuit pipeline toward the more advanced `detective_dog_ai` engine.
`detective_dog_ai` has uncommitted changes and new files around characters, scripts, and scheduling.
nextUp: Decide whether `detective_dog_ai` is now the canonical pipeline. If yes, say so explicitly in both root docs and subproject docs.
Run one clean end-to-end generation pass with documented prerequisites and capture the exact setup steps.
Add a top-level README comparing the two subprojects and explaining when each should be used.
blockers: The repo contains overlapping pipelines, which increases drift and makes it unclear which code path is authoritative.
Local model, asset, and API-key dependencies are implied by the code but are not documented at the root.
Because the root folder is not its own git repo, status can be missed unless agents also inspect the nested repos.

narrative.currentGoal: Consolidate the project direction around the strongest episode-generation pipeline and get one end-to-end content run working reliably.
narrative.inProgress: The project appears to be evolving from the simpler Biscuit pipeline toward the more advanced `detective_dog_ai` engine.

projects.name: verifyAI
projects.progressLabel: MVP frontend and backend are scaffolded, but product docs, testing, and operational polish are still thin
currentGoal: Mature the current proof of concept into a documented, testable product for evaluating challenge or verification-system resilience.
doneRecently: Built a React + Vite frontend with a landing page, input flow, result cards, and recommendation display.
Wired the frontend to call the backend and display the returned analysis, risk level, and captured image.
inProgress: The app is beyond a blank scaffold, but several pieces still read like a prototype:
frontend README is still the default Vite template
backend has no real test script
the operational setup is not documented from the root
nextUp: Replace the frontend template README with product-specific docs and add a root README.
Add one reproducible local run path that covers frontend, backend, and any model dependencies.
Add tests around score calculation and API-level validation, then record known limitations clearly.
blockers: The root project has no README, which makes handoff quality poor.
The git history is tied to the shared `/Users/aryansingh/repos` root rather than a dedicated repo.
The backend currently exposes only a narrow test flow and lacks confidence-building test coverage.

narrative.currentGoal: Mature the current proof of concept into a documented, testable product for evaluating challenge or verification-system resilience.
narrative.inProgress: The app is beyond a blank scaffold, but several pieces still read like a prototype:

projects.name: clipMaker
projects.progressLabel: folder exists but product scaffolding has not started
currentGoal: Clarify the actual product scope and create the first real project scaffold.
doneRecently: IDE metadata exists in `.idea/`, which suggests early setup work happened locally.
inProgress: No application code or runtime scaffold was found during inspection.
nextUp: Define the product in one paragraph: what `clipMaker` should do, for whom, and the preferred stack.
Create a minimal runnable scaffold and a README that explains local setup.
Decide whether this should become its own git repo instead of living only under the shared `/Users/aryansingh/repos` git root.
blockers: The folder name implies a video or editing tool, but there is not enough code yet to infer the intended architecture safely.
Without a README or entrypoint, future agents would have to guess and may pull the project in the wrong direction.
repoNotes: No `README`, `package.json`, `requirements.txt`, `pyproject.toml`, or source tree was found.

narrative.currentGoal: Clarify the actual product scope and create the first real project scaffold.
narrative.inProgress: No application code or runtime scaffold was found during inspection.
